title: tests: Skip link-local IPv4/IPv6 addresses in ipv{4,6}_get_addr_global()
body:
This PR:
- Fixes `ipv4_get_addr_global` & `ipv6_get_addr_global` by skipping link-local IPv4/IPv6 addresses as they are not globally routable.
- Fixes `has_ipv6` to also require a global IPv6 address.

TODO
- Solve issue with openQA s390x worker which has IPv6.

Otherwise we see this issue in openQA with IPv4 which uses TAP networking.  The openQA worker lacks IPv6 ATM.  This PR solves the issue for us.

```
not ok 569 [505] No IPv4 in 470ms
# tags: ci:parallel
# (from function `bail-now' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 187,
#  from function `assert' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 1062,
#  in test file test/system/505-networking-pasta.bats, line 300)
#   `assert "${container_address}" = "null" \' failed
#
# [17:42:37.016489330] $ /usr/bin/podman run --rm --net=pasta:-6 quay.io/libpod/testimage:20241011 ip -j -4 address show
# [17:42:37.184016463] [{"ifindex":1,"ifname":"lo","flags":["LOOPBACK","UP","LOWER_UP"],"mtu":65536,"qdisc":"noqueue","operstate":"UNKNOWN","group":"default","txqlen":1000,"addr_info":[{"family":"inet","local":"127.0.0.1","prefixlen":8,"scope":"host","label":"lo","valid_life_time":4294967295,"preferred_life_time":4294967295}]},{"ifindex":2,"ifname":"tap0","flags":["BROADCAST","MULTICAST","UP","LOWER_UP"],"mtu":65520,"qdisc":"pfifo_fast","operstate":"UNKNOWN","group":"default","txqlen":1000,"addr_info":[{"family":"inet","local":"169.254.2.1","prefixlen":16,"scope":"global","label":"tap0","valid_life_time":4294967295,"preferred_life_time":4294967295}]}]
# #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
# #|     FAIL: Container has IPv4 global address with IPv4 disabled
# #| expected: = null
# #|   actual:   169.254.2.1
# #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
# # [teardown]
```

```
not ok 576 [505] IPv6 default route in 543ms
# tags: ci:parallel
# (from function `bail-now' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 187,
#  from function `assert' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 1062,
#  in test file test/system/505-networking-pasta.bats, line 387)
#   `assert "${container_route}" = "${host_route}" \' failed
#
# [17:42:41.168101271] $ /usr/bin/podman run --rm --net=pasta quay.io/libpod/testimage:20241011 ip -j -6 route show
# [17:42:41.343733630] [{"dst":"fe80::/64","dev":"ens4","protocol":"kernel","metric":256,"flags":[],"pref":"medium"}]
# #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
# #|     FAIL: Container route not matching host
# #| expected: = fe80::2
# #|   actual:   null
# #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
# # [teardown]
```

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

`[{"ifindex":1,"ifname":"lo","flags":["LOOPBACK","UP","LOWER_UP"],"mtu":65536,"operstate":"UNKNOWN","txqlen":1000,"addr_info":[{"family":"inet6","local":"::1","prefixlen":128,"scope":"host","noprefixroute":true,"valid_life_time":4294967295,"preferred_life_time":4294967295}]},{"ifindex":2,"ifname":"ens4","flags":["BROADCAST","MULTICAST","UP","LOWER_UP"],"mtu":1500,"operstate":"UP","txqlen":1000,"addr_info":[{"family":"inet6","local":"fec0::3d6c:9288:2708:8b4","prefixlen":64,"scope":"site","temporary":true,"dynamic":true,"valid_life_time":86166,"preferred_life_time":14166},{"family":"inet6","local":"fec0::5054:ff:fe12:3456","prefixlen":64,"scope":"site","dynamic":true,"mngtmpaddr":true,"noprefixroute":true,"valid_life_time":86166,"preferred_life_time":14166},{"family":"inet6","local":"fe80::5054:ff:fe12:3456","prefixlen":64,"scope":"link","noprefixroute":true,"valid_life_time":4294967295,"preferred_life_time":4294967295}]}]`

Output of `ip -6 -j route`:

`[{"dst":"fe80::/64","dev":"ens4","protocol":"kernel","metric":1024,"flags":[],"pref":"medium"},{"dst":"fec0::/64","dev":"ens4","protocol":"ra","metric":100,"flags":[],"pref":"medium"},{"dst":"default","gateway":"fe80::2","dev":"ens4","protocol":"ra","metric":100,"flags":[],"pref":"medium"}]`

With the current code I'm now only getting:

```
not ok 4 [505] IPv6 default address assignment in 617ms

# (from function `bail-now' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 187,
#  from function `assert' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 1062,
#  in test file test/system/505-networking-pasta.bats, line 312)
#   `assert "${container_address}" = "${host_address}" \' failed
#
# [22:27:38.616031262] $ /usr/bin/podman run --rm --net=pasta quay.io/libpod/testimage:20241011 ip -j -6 address show
# [22:27:38.908813413] [{"ifindex":1,"ifname":"lo","flags":["LOOPBACK","UP","LOWER_UP"],"mtu":65536,"operstate":"UNKNOWN","txqlen":1000,"addr_info":[{"family":"inet6","local":"::1","prefixlen":128,"scope":"host","protocol":"kernel_lo","valid_life_time":4294967295,"preferred_life_time":4294967295}]},{"ifindex":2,"ifname":"enc1","flags":["BROADCAST","MULTICAST","UP","LOWER_UP"],"mtu":65520,"operstate":"UNKNOWN","txqlen":1000,"addr_info":[{"family":"inet6","local":"2a07:de40:b203:12:5054:ff:fe83:e2ee","prefixlen":64,"scope":"global","nodad":true,"mngtmpaddr":true,"noprefixroute":true,"valid_life_time":4294967295,"preferred_life_time":4294967295},{"family":"inet6","local":"2a07:de40:b203:12:5cfe:5075:1740:4a64","prefixlen":64,"scope":"global","nodad":true,"valid_life_time":4294967295,"preferred_life_time":4294967295},{"family":"inet6","local":"fe80::d4f6:9bff:feac:2b09","prefixlen":64,"scope":"link","nodad":true,"tentative":true,"protocol":"kernel_ll","valid_life_time":4294967295,"preferred_life_time":4294967295}]}]
# #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
# #|     FAIL: Container address not matching host
# #| expected: = 2a07:de40:b203:12:5cfe:5075:1740:4a64
# #|   actual:   2a07:de40:b203:12:5054:ff:fe83:e2ee

```

https://openqa.suse.de/tests/18737590/file/podman-ip4-addr.txt

`[{"ifindex":1,"ifname":"lo","flags":["LOOPBACK","UP","LOWER_UP"],"mtu":65536,"qdisc":"noqueue","operstate":"UNKNOWN","group":"default","txqlen":1000,"addr_info":[{"family":"inet","local":"127.0.0.1","prefixlen":8,"scope":"host","label":"lo","valid_life_time":4294967295,"preferred_life_time":4294967295}]},{"ifindex":2,"ifname":"enc1","flags":["BROADCAST","MULTICAST","UP","LOWER_UP"],"mtu":1500,"qdisc":"pfifo_fast","operstate":"UP","group":"default","txqlen":1000,"altnames":["enx52540083e2ee"],"addr_info":[{"family":"inet","local":"10.145.10.29","prefixlen":24,"broadcast":"10.145.10.255","scope":"global","dynamic":true,"noprefixroute":true,"label":"enc1","valid_life_time":13867,"preferred_life_time":13867}]}]`

https://openqa.suse.de/tests/18737590/file/podman-ip6-route.txt

`[{"dst":"2a07:de40:b203:12::/64","dev":"enc1","protocol":"ra","metric":100,"flags":[],"pref":"medium"},{"dst":"fe80::/64","dev":"enc1","protocol":"kernel","metric":1024,"flags":[],"pref":"medium"},{"dst":"default","gateway":"fe80::209:fff:fe09:13","dev":"enc1","protocol":"ra","metric":100,"flags":[],"pref":"medium"}]`

I'm closing this PR to open an issue.  I think the whole approach of using `ip route` and `ip addr` in `ipv4_get_addr_global`, `ipv6_get_addr_global` & `default_addr` is broken.  This information is not realiable and the kernel may make other routing decisions.
